heading: TAX RATE OF UNIT IMPOSING ADDITIONAL SALES AND USE TAX
operative_text: In the first year in which an additional sales and use tax is required to be collected, the no-new-revenue tax rate and voter-approval tax rate for the taxing unit are calculated according to the following formulas:

defined_terms.definition_excerpt: a number expressed in dollars per $100 of taxable value, calculated by dividing the revenue that will be generated by the additional sales and use tax in the following year as calculated under Subsection (d) by the current total value

defined_terms.term: last year's maintenance and operations expense
defined_terms.definition_excerpt: the amount spent for maintenance and operations from property tax and additional sales and use tax revenues in the preceding year

a number expressed in dollars per $100 of taxable value, calculated by dividing the revenue that will be generated by the additional sales and use tax in the current year as calculated under Subsection (d) by the current total value

a number expressed in dollars per $100 of taxable value, calculated by dividing the amount of sales and use tax revenue generated in the last four quarters for which the information is available by the current total value

_note: Section contains multiple subsections with formulas for no-new-revenue and voter-approval tax rates when a taxing unit imposes, continues, or ceases an additional sales and use tax. Subsection (c-1) was repealed.
